name: Thermoplastic Elastomer Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Thermoplastic Elastomer. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Thermoplastic Elastomer surface cleaning without thermal damage

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength optimized for Thermoplastic Elastomer absorption and minimal substrate damage

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning control on Thermoplastic Elastomer surfaces

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient cleaning with thermal management

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective contaminant removal without damaging Thermoplastic Elastomer substrate

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for controlled ablation of surface contaminants

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning coverage

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for complete contaminant removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for uniform cleaning without excessive thermal buildup

dwellTime.description: Optimal dwell time per spot for effective cleaning

power.description: Typical range for polymer cleaning to avoid thermal damage; starting at 30W for light contamination on TPE, max below melting threshold (~150-200°C); based on IPG Photonics specs for YLR series lasers and studies in Optics and Lasers in Engineering (2018).

scan_speed.description: Higher speeds minimize heat accumulation on heat-sensitive TPE; 2000 mm/s optimal for effective contaminant removal without substrate melting; ranges adjust for light (faster) vs heavy contamination; from industry manuals like those from Laserax and Applied Optics journal (2020).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred over ps/fs for cost-effective cleaning of polymers; 20 ns minimizes HAZ by rapid energy delivery before conduction; TPE's low thermal conductivity (~0.2 W/mK) benefits short pulses; cited in Laser & Photonics Reviews (2019) on polymer ablation.

repetition_rate.description: Balanced rate for TPE to enable fast cleaning while limiting heat buildup; higher rates increase speed but risk accumulation on low-melting TPE; trade-off noted in academic literature like Journal of Applied Physics (2021).

energy_density.description: Fluence below TPE damage threshold (~1-2 J/cm² at 1064 nm due to moderate absorption); 0.5 J/cm² safe for contaminant removal; conservative for reflectivity (~0.9) and low melting point; from ablation threshold studies in Applied Surface Science (2017).

spot_size.description: 200 μm recommended for uniform cleaning on TPE surfaces; smaller for fine details, larger for broad areas; suits industrial prep without focusing heat; per equipment specs from Coherent Inc. and process guidelines in Industrial Laser Solutions (2022).

pass_count.description: Multi-pass for heavy contamination on TPE to stay under thermal limits; 2 passes typical for complete removal without damage; single pass for light soils; based on case studies in Journal of Cleaner Production (2020) for polymer surface prep.

overlap_ratio.description: 60% ensures uniform coverage on irregular TPE surfaces; higher for heavy contamination to avoid gaps; standard range optimizes energy distribution; from process optimization in Lasers in Manufacturing and Materials Processing (2019).
